note: Mathlib/Probability/Distributions/Poisson/Basic.lean:41 — def poissonMeasure (r : ℝ≥0) : Measure ℕ, the Poisson distribution on ℕ (already @[wikidata Q205692]-tagged to 'Poisson distribution'). It is the marginal count law used inside a Poisson point process but Mathlib has no spatial point-process / random-counting-measure structure, so this is only a building block, not the process itself.
verify_note: Decl confirmed at exact cited line (def poissonMeasure (r : ℝ≥0) : Measure ℕ, already @[wikidata Q205692]); Q1145117 is Wikidata 'Poisson process' (subclass of stochastic process / counting process, points on a space), genuinely broader than the ℕ-valued count law — match_kind 'related'/low is honest, not overclaimed as 'exact'.
